introduction: This spec defines the requirements for the `web/` frontend scaffold of af-hub: a Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui project structure that establishes toolchain, dev proxy, build targets, and a minimal hello-world route, ready for future functional pages to be added incrementally. Note: requirements referencing shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, and React Router APIs are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against installed library versions before implementation.

glossary./healthz: The backend health probe HTTP endpoint defined by the `server_foundation` spec, forwarded by the Vite dev proxy.
glossary./readyz: The backend readiness probe HTTP endpoint defined by the `server_foundation` spec, forwarded by the Vite dev proxy.
glossary.@/: A TypeScript and Vite path alias that maps to the `src/` directory, used for all module imports within the project.
glossary.BrowserRouter: React Router's browser-history-based routing provider component used to enable client-side navigation.
glossary.Button: The shadcn/ui Button component, the first component copied into the source tree to validate the shadcn/ui setup end-to-end.
glossary.QueryClientProvider: The TanStack Query context provider component that must wrap the application to enable async data-fetching hooks in child components.
glossary.__APP_VERSION__: A compile-time constant injected by Vite's `define` mechanism from `process.env.npm_package_version`, representing the application version from `package.json`.
glossary.af-hub: The platform for which this web UI is being built; the backend is a Go server defined by the `server_foundation` spec.
glossary.changeOrigin: A Vite dev proxy option that rewrites the `Host` header of proxied requests to match the target server's hostname.
glossary.cn(): A utility function combining `clsx` and `tailwind-merge` for conditional Tailwind CSS class merging; generated by `npx shadcn-ui init` into `src/lib/utils.ts`.
glossary.components.json: Configuration file generated by `npx shadcn-ui init` that records style, theme, alias, and component path settings for the shadcn/ui CLI; must be committed to the repository.
glossary.darkMode: 'class': A Tailwind CSS configuration option that enables dark mode by toggling a `.dark` class on a parent element, as required by the shadcn/ui theming convention.
glossary.eslint.config.js: The ESLint flat config file (ESLint 9+ format) for the web project.
glossary.flat config format: The ESLint configuration format introduced in ESLint 9 using `eslint.config.js` (a JavaScript module exporting an array of config objects) instead of the legacy `.eslintrc.*` format.
glossary.globals.css: The global CSS entry point at `src/globals.css` containing Tailwind directives and shadcn/ui CSS variable declarations.
glossary.index.html: The Vite HTML entry point file located at the `web/` root (not inside `src/`), which loads the React application.
glossary.make web-build: A Makefile phony target that produces a production build via `cd web && npm run build`.
glossary.make web-dev: A Makefile phony target that starts the Vite development server via `cd web && npm run dev`.
glossary.make web-lint: A Makefile phony target that runs ESLint and TypeScript type checking via `cd web && npm run lint`.
glossary.noUncheckedIndexedAccess: A TypeScript strict compiler option that adds `undefined` to index signature results, preventing unsafe array/object access.
glossary.npm_package_version: An environment variable populated by npm when invoking scripts via `npm run`, containing the version field from `package.json`.
glossary.nvm: Node Version Manager, a tool for managing multiple Node.js versions; reads `.nvmrc` to select the correct version.
glossary.package-lock.json: The npm lockfile that records exact resolved versions of all dependencies; must be committed to ensure reproducible installs.
glossary.postcss.config.js: The PostCSS configuration file enabling Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer as PostCSS plugins.
glossary.shadcn/ui: A library of accessible, composable UI components copied directly into the project source tree via the shadcn-ui CLI, giving full control over component internals.
glossary.src/App.tsx: The root React component that sets up `BrowserRouter` (or equivalent) and `QueryClientProvider` wrapping all routes.
glossary.src/lib/utils.ts: The `cn()` helper module generated by `npx shadcn-ui init`, combining `clsx` and `tailwind-merge`; required by all shadcn/ui component copies.
glossary.src/main.tsx: The React entry point that mounts the root `<App />` component into the `#root` DOM element.
glossary.src/routes/index.tsx: The route component rendered at `/`; displays the af-hub heading, app version, and a shadcn/ui Button.
glossary.src/routes/not-found.tsx: The 404 catch-all route component rendered for any unmatched path; displays a human-readable message and a link back to `/`.
glossary.tailwind.config.ts: The Tailwind CSS configuration file defining content paths, theme extensions, and dark mode strategy.
glossary.tsconfig.json: The TypeScript compiler configuration file for browser-side source files under `src/`.
glossary.tsconfig.node.json: A secondary TypeScript configuration file covering Node-side config files such as `vite.config.ts`.
glossary.verbatim path forwarding: Proxy behaviour where the request path is forwarded to the target unchanged, without any rewriting or stripping of path segments.
glossary.vite-env.d.ts: The Vite ambient type declaration file at `src/vite-env.d.ts` that declares `__APP_VERSION__` and includes Vite client types.
glossary.vite.config.ts: The Vite build tool configuration file for the web project, defining plugins, dev server settings, proxy rules, and path aliases.
glossary.web/: The frontend project directory at the repository root containing all web UI source files and configuration.
glossary.web/.nvmrc: A file pinning the Node.js version for the web project, read by nvm (Node Version Manager) to select the correct runtime.
glossary.web/dist/: The output directory produced by `npm run build` containing the compiled production-ready frontend assets.
glossary.web/node_modules/: The directory where npm installs all frontend package dependencies; must not be committed.

user_story.goal: have a pinned Node.js version and committed lockfile
user_story.benefit: all contributors and CI environments use identical runtimes and exact dependency versions

acceptance_criteria.action: commits `web/.nvmrc` containing the string `22` to pin Node.js 22 LTS as the canonical runtime version
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: File `web/.nvmrc` exists in the repository with content `22`

acceptance_criteria.action: commits `package-lock.json` at `web/package-lock.json` and keeps it up-to-date so that `npm ci` produces a reproducible install
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `web/package-lock.json` is present in the repository and consistent with `web/package.json`

acceptance_criteria.action: specifies all dependency versions using semver caret ranges (`^`), with the `package-lock.json` serving as the source of truth for exact resolved versions
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All version strings in `package.json` `dependencies` and `devDependencies` use `^` prefix

edge_cases.action: the setup is considered incomplete; developers using `nvm` will select the wrong Node.js version, breaking the environment parity guarantee
edge_cases.error_condition: `web/.nvmrc` is absent or contains a version other than `22`

user_story.goal: have build artifacts and installed packages excluded from version control
user_story.benefit: the repository stays clean and CI does not accidentally commit generated files

acceptance_criteria.action: includes the entries `web/dist/` and `web/node_modules/` so that build output and installed packages are never tracked by git
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Running `git check-ignore web/dist/ web/node_modules/` returns both paths as ignored

acceptance_criteria.action: contains only these two web-specific ignore rules; no separate `web/.gitignore` is created by this spec

requirements.title: Project directory structure and file initialization

user_story.goal: have a fully initialized `web/` directory with all required files committed
user_story.benefit: any contributor can clone the repo and immediately run `npm install` followed by `npm run dev` without manual setup steps

acceptance_criteria.action: commits all required top-level files: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.node.json`, `vite.config.ts`, `tailwind.config.ts`, `postcss.config.js`, `eslint.config.js`, `components.json`, `index.html`, `.nvmrc`, and `package-lock.json`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All listed files exist in the repository under `web/`

acceptance_criteria.action: contains `main.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `globals.css`, `vite-env.d.ts`, `lib/utils.ts`, `routes/index.tsx`, `routes/not-found.tsx`, and the `components/ui/` directory with at least the `button.tsx` component copy
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All listed paths exist under `web/src/` in the repository

acceptance_criteria.action: mounts `<App />` into the DOM element with id `root` using React 18's `createRoot` API
is located at `web/index.html` (the Vite project root, not inside `src/`) and references `src/main.tsx` as the module entry point
is generated by `npx shadcn-ui init` and committed at `web/components.json`, recording the style, theme, alias, and component path configuration for future `npx shadcn-ui add` invocations
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: File `web/components.json` exists and is valid JSON parseable by the shadcn/ui CLI

acceptance_criteria.action: exports the `cn()` helper function combining `clsx` and `tailwind-merge`, as generated by `npx shadcn-ui init`; the file must not be deleted or omitted
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Importing `cn` from `@/lib/utils` in TypeScript resolves without error and the function is callable

edge_cases.action: future `npx shadcn-ui add <component>` commands will fail or prompt for re-initialization, breaking incremental component adoption
all shadcn/ui component copies (including `Button`) fail to compile because their imports of `cn()` cannot be resolved

user_story.goal: have all required npm packages declared in `package.json`
user_story.benefit: a clean `npm install` provides all necessary runtime and tooling dependencies without manual package additions

acceptance_criteria.action: declares `react`, `react-dom`, `react-router-dom`, and `@tanstack/react-query` as runtime dependencies using semver caret ranges
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All four packages are listed under `dependencies` in `web/package.json`

acceptance_criteria.action: declares `typescript`, `vite`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `tailwindcss`, `postcss`, `autoprefixer`, `eslint` (v9+), `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`, `@typescript-eslint/parser`, `eslint-plugin-react`, and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` as dev dependencies using semver caret ranges
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All listed packages are present under `devDependencies` in `web/package.json`

acceptance_criteria.action: defines `dev`, `build`, and `lint` npm scripts: `dev` starts the Vite dev server, `build` produces a production build to `web/dist/`, and `lint` runs ESLint and TypeScript type checking
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `npm run dev`, `npm run build`, and `npm run lint` are all valid script invocations

user_story.goal: have strict TypeScript settings configured for all source files
user_story.benefit: type errors are caught at compile time across the entire frontend codebase, preventing runtime bugs

acceptance_criteria.action: enables `strict: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`, `moduleResolution: bundler`, `target: ES2022`, `lib: [ES2022, DOM, DOM.Iterable]`, `jsx: react-jsx`, `module: ESNext`, `skipLibCheck: true`, `noEmit: true`, `baseUrl: .`, and `paths: { @/*: [./src/*] }`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `tsc --noEmit` exits with code 0 on a clean scaffold

acceptance_criteria.action: is committed at `web/tsconfig.node.json` with `composite: true`, `skipLibCheck: true`, `module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: bundler`, `allowSyntheticDefaultImports: true`, and `include: [vite.config.ts]` to cover Node-side config files
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `vite.config.ts` type-checks without errors when referenced by `tsconfig.node.json`

acceptance_criteria.action: applies to all TypeScript files under `web/src/` as determined by the `include` or `rootDir` configuration
declares the ambient constant `__APP_VERSION__: string` so TypeScript resolves the injected Vite define value without error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Referencing `__APP_VERSION__` in any `src/` file produces no TypeScript error

edge_cases.action: unsafe array and object index access goes undetected by the compiler, violating the strict type-safety requirement of this project

user_story.goal: use the `@/` alias to import modules relative to `src/`
user_story.benefit: imports remain clean and stable regardless of file nesting depth, and shadcn/ui component copies resolve their own internal imports correctly

acceptance_criteria.action: configures `compilerOptions.paths` with `@/*` mapping to `./src/*` so TypeScript resolves `@/`-prefixed imports
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: TypeScript resolves `import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'` without error

acceptance_criteria.action: configures `resolve.alias` mapping `@` to `path.resolve(__dirname, './src')` so Vite resolves `@/`-prefixed imports at bundle time
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: The development server and production build both resolve `@/` imports correctly

acceptance_criteria.action: use the `@/` alias (e.g., `import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'`) rather than relative paths

edge_cases.action: module resolution succeeds in one tool but fails in the other, causing either type errors or runtime bundle failures; both configurations must be kept in sync
edge_cases.error_condition: `@/` is configured in `tsconfig.json` but not in `vite.config.ts` (or vice versa)

user_story.goal: have ESLint 9+ flat config with TypeScript and React rules applied to all source files
user_story.benefit: lint errors are caught early in the development loop, and false positives (like `react/react-in-jsx-scope`) do not block valid code

acceptance_criteria.action: uses the ESLint 9+ flat config format (a JavaScript module exporting an array of config objects) and is located at `web/eslint.config.js`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: ESLint loads `eslint.config.js` without errors when invoked from `web/`

acceptance_criteria.action: includes TypeScript-aware linting by configuring `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` and `@typescript-eslint/parser`
includes React-specific rules via `eslint-plugin-react` and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`
explicitly disables the `react/react-in-jsx-scope` rule to avoid false-positive errors caused by the React 17+ automatic JSX transform (`jsx: react-jsx`)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: No `react/react-in-jsx-scope` errors appear when `npm run lint` is executed

acceptance_criteria.action: produces zero lint errors when `npm run lint` is executed on the initial committed state
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `npm run lint` exits with code 0

acceptance_criteria.action: runs both `eslint .` and `tsc --noEmit` so that lint checking covers both ESLint rules and TypeScript type correctness
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `npm run lint` exits with code 0 on a clean scaffold with zero errors from either tool

edge_cases.action: React-specific rule violations (e.g., rules-of-hooks violations) go undetected; both plugins are required, not optional
edge_cases.error_condition: `eslint-plugin-react` or `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` is omitted from the config

user_story.goal: have Tailwind CSS configured with shadcn/ui CSS variable theming
user_story.benefit: shadcn/ui components render with correct styles out of the box and future component additions automatically inherit the design system

acceptance_criteria.action: includes `@tailwind base`, `@tailwind components`, and `@tailwind utilities` directives, plus the shadcn/ui CSS variable declarations for `:root` and `.dark` pseudo-classes (e.g., `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`) as generated by `npx shadcn-ui init`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: The Tailwind directives compile without errors and CSS variables are present in the output stylesheet

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `content` to `['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}']`, extends the theme with shadcn/ui CSS variable colour mappings (e.g., `colors: { background: 'hsl(var(--background))', ... }`), and sets `darkMode` to `'class'`
configures `tailwindcss` and `autoprefixer` as PostCSS plugins so Tailwind directives are processed during the build
imports `globals.css` to ensure Tailwind styles and shadcn/ui CSS variables are applied globally to the application
is copied into `src/components/ui/button.tsx` via `npx shadcn-ui add button` (or equivalent) and renders with correct Tailwind-based styles when used in the hello-world route
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: The `Button` component renders visually styled output in the browser without CSS errors

edge_cases.action: Tailwind purges all utility classes from the production build, causing the UI to render unstyled; `content` must include `./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`
shadcn/ui components render without correct colours because `hsl(var(--background))` and similar references resolve to empty values
edge_cases.error_condition: shadcn/ui CSS variable declarations are absent from `globals.css`

requirements.title: Vite dev server and proxy configuration

user_story.goal: have the Vite dev server proxy API and health check requests to the Go backend
user_story.benefit: frontend development works seamlessly with the backend without CORS issues or manual URL switching, and frontend-only development is unblocked when the backend is not running

acceptance_criteria.action: configures the dev server to listen on port 5173 (the Vite default) without a custom port override
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `npm run dev` binds the dev server to `http://localhost:5173`

acceptance_criteria.action: forwards `/api/*` requests to `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true` and verbatim path forwarding (no path rewriting)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: A request to `http://localhost:5173/api/example` is proxied as-is to `http://localhost:8080/api/example`

acceptance_criteria.action: forwards `/healthz` requests to `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true` and verbatim path forwarding
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: A request to `http://localhost:5173/healthz` is proxied to `http://localhost:8080/healthz`

acceptance_criteria.action: forwards `/readyz` requests to `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true` and verbatim path forwarding
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: A request to `http://localhost:5173/readyz` is proxied to `http://localhost:8080/readyz`

acceptance_criteria.action: the dev server starts successfully on port 5173; proxied requests to the backend fail silently with a connection-refused error, which is expected and acceptable during frontend-only development
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `npm run dev` starts without error regardless of backend availability
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the Go backend at `http://localhost:8080` is not running when the Vite dev server starts

edge_cases.action: the proxied path sent to the backend differs from the original request path, breaking API and health check endpoints; verbatim forwarding must be enforced by omitting any `rewrite` configuration
edge_cases.error_condition: path rewriting is accidentally configured on any proxy rule

user_story.goal: invoke web development, build, and lint tasks from the root Makefile
user_story.benefit: all project tasks are accessible from a single consistent interface without needing to `cd` into `web/` manually

acceptance_criteria.action: adds `make web-dev` as a `.PHONY` target that runs `cd web && npm run dev`, starting the Vite dev server on port 5173 with hot reload
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `make web-dev` starts the dev server on port 5173

acceptance_criteria.action: adds `make web-build` as a `.PHONY` target that runs `cd web && npm run build`, producing a production build to `web/dist/`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `make web-build` exits with code 0 and `web/dist/` contains built assets

acceptance_criteria.action: adds `make web-lint` as a `.PHONY` target that runs `cd web && npm run lint`, executing ESLint and TypeScript type checking
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `make web-lint` exits with code 0 on a clean scaffold

acceptance_criteria.action: declares all three targets (`web-dev`, `web-build`, `web-lint`) in the `.PHONY` list alongside any pre-existing phony targets
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Running `make -n web-dev web-build web-lint` reports all three as phony targets

user_story.goal: see a working hello-world page at `/` that demonstrates the full toolchain integration
user_story.benefit: every major piece of the scaffold (React, routing, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, app version injection) is validated end-to-end before functional pages are built

acceptance_criteria.action: renders a heading displaying the text `af-hub`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: The DOM contains a heading element with the text `af-hub`

acceptance_criteria.action: displays the application version by reading the `__APP_VERSION__` constant injected by Vite's `define` mechanism from `process.env.npm_package_version`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: The rendered page shows the version string from `package.json` when the app is started via `npm run dev`

acceptance_criteria.action: renders at least one shadcn/ui `Button` component imported via `import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'` as a non-functional placeholder to validate the shadcn/ui integration
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: The `Button` component renders visually in the browser without import errors or style failures

acceptance_criteria.action: defines `__APP_VERSION__` via the Vite `define` option as `JSON.stringify(process.env.npm_package_version)` so the version is injected at build time
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `__APP_VERSION__` is a string constant available in all `src/` TypeScript files

acceptance_criteria.action: sets up React Router client-side routing (using `BrowserRouter` or `createBrowserRouter`) and registers the hello-world route (`src/routes/index.tsx`) at path `/`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Navigating to `http://localhost:5173/` renders the hello-world component

acceptance_criteria.action: wraps the router with `QueryClientProvider` (from `@tanstack/react-query`) so all child components can use TanStack Query hooks in future specs
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `QueryClientProvider` is present in the React component tree wrapping all routes

edge_cases.action: `process.env.npm_package_version` is `undefined` and `__APP_VERSION__` is injected as the string `'undefined'`; developers must use npm scripts to ensure correct version injection
edge_cases.error_condition: Vite is invoked directly (e.g., `npx vite`) rather than via `npm run dev` or `npm run build`

user_story.goal: see a clear 404 message when navigating to an undefined path
user_story.benefit: unmatched URLs produce a helpful error page rather than a blank screen or silent failure

acceptance_criteria.action: registers a catch-all route using the wildcard path (`path='*'` or equivalent for `createBrowserRouter`) that renders the component at `src/routes/not-found.tsx`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Any path other than `/` renders the not-found component

acceptance_criteria.action: displays a human-readable message such as `404 — Page not found` and a clickable link back to `/` using React Router's `Link` component
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: The rendered page contains a 404 message and a navigable link to `/`

edge_cases.action: unmatched paths render a blank page or throw a React Router error; the wildcard route must always be the last entry in the route configuration
edge_cases.error_condition: the catch-all route is absent from the router configuration

correctness_properties.title: Zero lint errors on clean scaffold
correctness_properties.invariant: `npm run lint` (which runs `eslint .` and `tsc --noEmit`) exits with code 0 — no ESLint errors and no TypeScript type errors

correctness_properties.title: Path alias consistency across TypeScript and Vite
correctness_properties.for_any: import statement using the `@/` prefix in any file under `web/src/`
correctness_properties.invariant: the import resolves successfully in both `tsc --noEmit` (via `tsconfig.json` `paths`) and the Vite bundler (via `vite.config.ts` `resolve.alias`), with no discrepancy between the two

correctness_properties.title: Proxy verbatim forwarding
correctness_properties.for_any: HTTP request whose path begins with `/api`, or exactly matches `/healthz` or `/readyz`, made to the Vite dev server
correctness_properties.invariant: the path forwarded to `http://localhost:8080` is identical to the original request path — no segments are stripped, rewritten, or appended

correctness_properties.title: shadcn/ui CLI re-invocability
correctness_properties.for_any: execution of `npx shadcn-ui add <component>` in the `web/` directory after the scaffold is committed
correctness_properties.invariant: the command succeeds without re-running `npx shadcn-ui init` because `components.json` is present, valid, and consistent with the alias and path configuration in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`

correctness_properties.title: Build output reproducibility
correctness_properties.for_any: execution of `npm ci && npm run build` in the `web/` directory on any machine
correctness_properties.invariant: the build succeeds and produces output in `web/dist/`, using exact dependency versions from `package-lock.json`, with `__APP_VERSION__` injected from `npm_package_version`

execution_paths.title: Developer starts frontend dev server and views hello-world page

steps.action: runs `make web-dev` (or `npm run dev`) from the repository root
executes `cd web && npm run dev`, delegating to the `dev` script in `package.json`
starts on port 5173, loads `vite.config.ts`, registers the proxy rules for `/api`, `/healthz`, and `/readyz`, and begins serving the app with hot module replacement
navigates to `http://localhost:5173/`, triggering a request to the Vite dev server
serves `index.html` which loads `src/main.tsx` as the module entry point
mounts `<App />` into `#root`, rendering `QueryClientProvider`, `BrowserRouter`, and routing to `src/routes/index.tsx` for the `/` path
displays the hello-world page showing the `af-hub` heading, the application version string from `__APP_VERSION__`, and a styled shadcn/ui `Button` component

execution_paths.title: Developer produces a production build via Makefile

steps.action: runs `make web-build` from the repository root
executes `cd web && npm run build`, delegating to the `build` script in `package.json`

bundles all TypeScript and CSS files, resolves `@/` path aliases, injects `__APP_VERSION__` from `process.env.npm_package_version`, applies Tailwind CSS purging via PostCSS, and writes output to `web/dist/`
observes that `web/dist/` contains the compiled HTML, JS, and CSS assets ready for deployment

execution_paths.title: Developer runs lint and type checking via Makefile

steps.action: runs `make web-lint` from the repository root
executes `cd web && npm run lint`, which runs `eslint . && tsc --noEmit`
loads `eslint.config.js` (flat config), applies TypeScript-aware rules and React rules to all source files, enforces that `react/react-in-jsx-scope` is disabled, and reports any violations
runs `tsc --noEmit` using `tsconfig.json`, checking all files under `web/src/` for type errors without emitting output
receives exit code 0 and no error output, confirming the scaffold is lint- and type-clean

execution_paths.title: Browser navigates to undefined path and sees 404 page

steps.action: navigates to `http://localhost:5173/nonexistent-page` in the browser
serves `index.html` (SPA fallback) because the path does not match any static file
evaluates the path `/nonexistent-page` against the registered routes; no match is found for `/`, so the wildcard catch-all route `path='*'` matches
renders the `src/routes/not-found.tsx` component, displaying `404 — Page not found` and a link to `/`
clicks the link back to `/` and is navigated to the hello-world route via React Router client-side navigation

execution_paths.title: Browser API request proxied to Go backend

steps.action: makes an HTTP request to `http://localhost:5173/api/example` from within the React app
matches the `/api` prefix rule in `vite.config.ts` and forwards the request verbatim to `http://localhost:8080/api/example` with `changeOrigin: true`
receives the request at `http://localhost:8080/api/example` and responds
relays the backend response back to the browser, transparent to the frontend code

error_handling.condition: Go backend at `http://localhost:8080` is not running when a proxied request (to `/api`, `/healthz`, or `/readyz`) is made
error_handling.behavior: The Vite dev proxy returns a connection-refused error to the browser; the dev server itself continues running on port 5173 unaffected. This is expected during frontend-only development.

error_handling.condition: `process.env.npm_package_version` is `undefined` because Vite is invoked directly instead of via `npm run dev` or `npm run build`
error_handling.behavior: `__APP_VERSION__` is injected as the string `'undefined'` and displayed on the hello-world page. Developers must use npm scripts to ensure correct version injection.
Future `npx shadcn-ui add <component>` invocations fail or prompt for re-initialization. The file must be committed after `npx shadcn-ui init` to prevent this.
All shadcn/ui component copies (including `Button`) fail to compile because their `cn()` imports cannot be resolved. The file must always be committed.

error_handling.condition: `npm run lint` reports one or more ESLint or TypeScript errors on the initial scaffold
error_handling.behavior: The lint step exits with a non-zero code. The scaffold must be corrected until `npm run lint` exits with code 0 before the spec is considered complete.

error_handling.condition: The `@/` alias is configured in `tsconfig.json` but not in `vite.config.ts`
error_handling.behavior: TypeScript type checking passes but the Vite bundler cannot resolve `@/`-prefixed imports at runtime, causing the dev server and build to fail with module-not-found errors. Both configurations must always be kept in sync.
